title: rhododendron procumbens
intro: Rhododendron procumbens, also known as rosebay rhododendron or rhododendron ponticum, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its clusters of pink or white flowers and elliptical leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high concentration of salicylic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of rhododendron procumbens include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Rhododendron procumbens can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
